matcher.subject: Mathematics
matcher.coveragePreview: surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes grade level mathematics students surface areas of 3d shapes volumes of 3d shapes unit conversion in geometry surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes introduction to session topic session: surface areas & volumes (3-d shapes) key focus: cube, cuboid, cylinder, cone, sphere what we will learn today learning objectives identify properties of cube, cuboid, cylinder, cone, sphere calculate total surface area (tsa) using correct formulas compute volume for all standard 3d shapes recalling key properties shape identification & dimensions cube: all sides equal (a) cuboid: length (l), width (w), height (h) circular shapes: radius (r) or diameter (d) total surface area: cuboids & cubes surface area formulas - rectangular solids cuboid tsa = 2(lw + wh + hl) cube tsa = 6a² (all faces are squares) lateral surface area excludes top/bottom curved surfaces & spheres surface area formulas - curved solids cylinder tsa = 2πr(h + r) cone tsa = πr(l + r), where l is slant height sphere surface area = 4πr² example: calculating for a cube application of formulas - cubes [object object] [object object] volume formula: a³ → 5³ = 125 cm³ practice: calculating cylinder...
